request.question: Kalyan is known as what in West Asia?

title: 2011 AFC Champions League group stage
Kalyani (raga)
Haplogroup I (mtDNA)
Talmiz Ahmad
2011 AFC Cup group stage
Yaman (raga)
Ethnic groups in West Asia
List of Ultras of West Asia
West Asian Tennis Federation
West Asia Basketball Association
sentences: A total of 32 teams, 16 from West Asia and 16 from East Asia, competed in the 2011 AFC Champions League group stage.
They included 30 direct entries and 2 winners of the qualifying play-off (one from West Asia and one from East Asia).
Kalyani or Kalyan, alternatively called Yaman in Hindustani Music, is a melakarta raga (parent musical scale) in the Carnatic music.
Its Western equivalent is the Lydian mode.
Haplogroup I is a human mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) haplogroup.
It is largely distributed in Europe and West Asia, and is also found among some Afroasiatic-speaking populations in Africa.
The clade is believed to have evolved in West Asia (see Origin and Distribution), i.e. the Near East during the LGM period .
He has published two books: Reform in the Arab World: External Influences and Regional Debates (2005) and Children of Abraham at War: the Clash of Messianic Militarisms (2010).
His new book, "The Islamist Challenge in West Asia: Doctrinal and Political Competitions" after the Arab Spring, published in August 2013.
He writes and lectures frequently on the politics of West Asia, Political Islam and energy security issues.
Presently he is a Visiting Distinguished Fellow at ORF.
A total of 32 teams, 20 from West Asia and 12 from East Asia, competed in the 2011 AFC Cup group stage.
They included 28 direct entries and 4 losers of the 2011 AFC Champions League qualifying play-off (two from West Asia and two from East Asia).
Yaman (also known as Emaan in West Asia and 'Kalyani' in Carnatic classical music) is a heptatonic (Sampurna) Hindustani Classical raga of Kalyan Thaat.
The ethnic groups in West Asia refers to the various peoples that reside in West Asia.
The region has historically been a crossroad of different cultures.

Since the 1960s, the changes in political and economic factors (especially the enormous oil wealth in the region and conflicts) have significantly altered the ethnic composition of groups in the region.
While some ethnic groups have been present in the region for millennia, others have arrived fairly recently through immigration.

The five largest ethnic groups in the region are Arabs, Azerbaijanis, Kurds, Persians, and Turks but there are dozens of other ethnic groups which have hundreds of thousands, if not millions, of members.
This is a list of all 90 of the ultra-prominent peaks (with topographic prominence greater than 1,500 metres) in West Asia.
It includes peaks on the islands of Cyprus and Socotra.
It does not include the 10 ultras of the Caucasus which are listed under Europe although they are geographically more connected to the mountains of West Asia.
The West Asian Tennis Federation (WATF) also known as ITF West Asia (International Tennis Federation West Asia) is a subzone of the ATF consisting of countries from West Asia.
The West Asia Basketball Association (WABA) is a subzone of FIBA Asia, consisting of countries from West Asia.
The WABA Champions Cup, a professional men's tournament, is a top level club competition run by the WABA.

response.hop1_query: Kalyan is known as what in West Asia?
hop1_docs: Yaman (raga) | Yaman (also known as Emaan in West Asia and 'Kalyani' in Carnatic classical music) is a heptatonic (Sampurna) Hindustani Classical raga of Kalyan Thaat.

T. S. Kalyanaraman | T. S. Kalyanaraman (born 1951) is an Indian businessman best known as the chairman and managing director of Kalyan Jewellers and Kalyan Developers.  Kalyan Group is the holding company of Kalyan Jewellers.

Kalyan Chaubey | Kalyan Chaubey (born 16 December 1976) or Kalyan Chaubey is an Indian footballer of Kolkata,West Bengal Kanaujia Aryan Brahmin descent who plays as a goalkeeper.  Twice Indian Goalkeeper of the year in 1997-98 and 2001-02.  Kalyan Chaubey has an younger sister named Bulbuli Panja.  Bulibuli is a professional television actor for Bengali TV Serials and a Football Anchor.  Kalyan Chaubey is married in 2004 and blessed with a daughter.

Kalyan Silks | Kalyan Silks was founded in Thrissur in 1909 as a store specializing in silk.  Today, it has 21 showrooms in India and the UAE, making it India’s largest silk saree showroom network.  Mr. T. S. Pattabhiraman is the Chairman and Managing Director of the group.  Under his leadership Kalyan Silks has been able to add at least one new showroom to their network every year.  He is also credited for redefining silk trade through innovative concepts that include introducing India’s first branded bridal silk saree, “Saugandhika” and being the first textile retailer to create in-house brands.  These innovations coupled with inventive retail practices is what has shaped Kalyan Silks’ success.  With a turnover of 1100 crores, 1000 weaving centers, 300 design centers, 100 production units and 4000 employees, the Kalyan Silks is a formidable force in textile retail.  Malayalam superstar Prithviraj Sukumaran has been the brand’s ambassador since 2011.

Kalyan West (Vidhan Sabha constituency) | Kalyan West Vidhan Sabha constituency (Marathi: कल्याण पश्चिम विधानसभा मतदारसंघ ) is one of the 288 Vidhan Sabha (legislative assembly) constituencies of Maharashtra state, western India.  This constituency is located in Thane district.

Kole-Kalyan railway station | Kole-Kalyan was a former station on the Salsette-Trombay Railway serving the East Indian village of Kole-Kalyan, now known as Kalina.  It was dismantled after the rail line closed down in 1934.
Nepali Kalyan High School | Nepali Kalyan High School is a co-educational school Siliguri, West Bengal, India.  It was founded in 1983 and is managed by the Department of Higher Education.

hop2_docs: Yaman (raga) | Yaman (also known as Emaan in West Asia and 'Kalyani' in Carnatic classical music) is a heptatonic (Sampurna) Hindustani Classical raga of Kalyan Thaat.

Emaan | Emaan is a 2011 Afghan action romance Drama film directed and produced by Haris Yosufi and starring Rasul Iman, Tamana Amini and Ghafar Qutbyar.  After much await and public demand, Emaan film was finally screened at Reading Cinemas in Australia.  This is the first time an Afghan Film to be screened at a Cinema.  Emaan - the winner of 2011 South Asian Film Festival (Canberra Australia) for Best Story and Best Film, where films like '3 Idiots' of India was also nominated and shown.  The film's director and Producer Haris Yosufi resident of Australia he travelled to Afghanistan and spent six months shooting his film.  He spent a further Five months in Australia for the post-production work.  the film was released on 27 February 2011 in Afghanistan.

Kalyan Mukherjee (politician) | Kalyan Mukherjee (Bengali: কল্যাণ মুখার্জি ) was the deputy mayor of Kolkata Municipal Corporation (KMC) in the city of Kolkata, West Bengal, India.  He was elected as a councillor from a ward in the North Kolkata.  He is a member of the Revolutionary Socialist Party which is a part of the Left Front.  As Deputy Mayor, Kalyan Mukherjee maintains the portfolio of Water Supply in Ships, Agency & Stores, Mayor In Council.
